title: On Gilly-Flowers Begotten.
content:
What was't that fell but now
    From that warm kiss of ours?
    Look, look! by love I vow
    They were two gilly-flowers.

    Let's kiss and kiss again,
    For if so be our closes
    Make gilly-flowers, then
    I'm sure they'll fashion roses.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "On Gilly-Flowers Begotten."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
